definition: data dictionary for electric/electronic components

industrial product which serves a specific function or functions, which in a given context is considered not decomposable or physically divisible and which is intended for use in a higher- order assembled product
discrete device or physical entity in an electric or electronic system used to affect electrons or their associated fields
active two-port device essentially designed to produce an output signal of greater power than that of the input signal
amplifier designed for use with baseband unmodulated signals
note: EXAMPLE...Audio,video and switching signals.

definition: amplifier optimized to deliver output power to the load
note: Normally the voltage gain is insignificant and the power gain is thus mainly due to current gain.

definition: amplifier designed primarily to amplify the signal voltage, without supplying appreciable power
amplifier whose output signal is proportional to the algebraic difference between the voltages applied to its two inputs
high-gain differential dc-coupled amplifier
differential alternating current- coupled amplifier
amplifier of which one input terminal is directly connected to earth
amplifier designed for use with signals modulated on a radio-frequency carrier
amplifier designed for uniform operation within a broad range of frequencies
note: The range of frequencies usually accommodates several signal channels.

definition: part of a radio transmitting or receiving system which is designed to provide the required coupling between a transmitter or a receiver and the medium in which the radio wave propagates
note:
Note 1...In practice, the terminals of the antenna or the points to be considered as the interface between the antenna and the transmitter or receiver should be specified. ||
Note 2...If a transmitter or receiver is connected to its antenna by a feed line, the antenna may be considered to be a transducer between the guided waves of the feed line and the radiated waves in space.

definition: antenna in which, under given conditions,the predominant function is effected by means of capacitance
antenna in which, under given conditions, the predominant function is effected by means of inductance.
antenna in which, under given conditions, the predominant function is effected by means of resistance
electrochemical energy source consisting of one or more cells
electrochemical system (battery) designed for delivery of electric energy in one single uninterrupted (continuous) or interrupted (intermittent) discharge
electrochemical system (battery) capable of storing in chemical form the electric energy received and which can give it back by reconversion
system of two conductors (plates) separated over the extent of its surfaces by a thin insulating medium (dielectric), its intended characteristic being capacitance
capacitor with a designed inherent fixed capacitance
note: Since capacitance is a function of temperature, it may still vary with temperature.

definition: fixed capacitor with a dielectric consisting of air
fixed capacitor with a dielectric consisting of a ceramic material

ceramic capacitor specially designed and suited for resonant circuit application where low losses and high stability of capacitance are essential or where a precisely defined temperature coefficient is required
remark: The ceramic dielectric is defined by its rated temperature coefficient (alpha).

definition: ceramic capacitor which has a dielectric with a high permittivity and which is suitable for by-pass and coupling applications or for frequency discriminating circuits where low losses and high stability of capacitance are not of major importance
remark: The ceramic dielectric is characterized by the non-linear change of capacitance over the category temperature range.

definition: fixed capacitor in which the electrolytically formed oxide layer on the surface of the anode serves as a dielectric and having a solid or non-solid electrolyte forming the cathode, usually having polar properties

fixed electrolytic capacitor in which the oxide film formed on the surface of foil, wire or sintered slug of tantalum by anodic oxidation is employed as the dielectric, and solid electrolyte in close contact with this dielectric is employed as a part of the cathode

fixed electrolytic capacitor in which the oxide formed on the surface of tantalum by anodic oxidation is employed as the dielectric and paper or fibre impregnated with liquid electrolyte and located in close contact with this dielectric is employed as part of the cathode

fixed electrolytic capacitor in which the oxide film formed on the surface of foil, wire or sintered slug of aluminium by anodic oxidation is employed as the dielectric, and solid electrolyte in close contact with this dielectric is employed as part of the cathode

fixed electrolytic capacitor in which the oxide formed on the surface of aluminium foil by anodic oxidation is employed as the dielectric and paper or fibre impregnated with liquid electrolyte and located in close contact with this dielectric is employed as part of the cathode
fixed capacitor with a dielectric consisting of a plastic film
fixed capacitor with a dielectric consisting of glass
fixed capacitor with a dielectric consisting of mica
fixed capacitor with a dielectric consisting of paper, usually impregnated
fixed capacitor in which the dielectric is formed from layers of two or more different materials
capacitor designed that its main property can be varied by mechanically changing the spatial relationship of their parts
element intended to carry electric current
note:
Note 1 – The term "conductor" is often used for an element the length of which is large with respect to the cross-sectional dimensions, e.g. conductors of a line or of a cable. |
Note 2 – The English term "conductor" and the French term "conducteur" have also the meaning of "conducting medium". |
Note 3 – In French, the term "conducteur" is also used as an adjective corresponding to the English "conductive".

definition: conductor consisting of one or more uninsulated electric conductive elements
note: Note 1...The term "conductor" is often used for an element the length of which is large with respect to the cross-sectional dimensions, e.g. conductors of a line or of a cable. |Note 2...The English term "conductor" and the French term "conducteur" have also the meaning of "conducting medium".

definition: conductor containing one or more insulated conductive parts
assembly of one or more conductors and/or optical fibres, with a protective covering and possibly filling, insulating and protective material
cable designed for the purpose of transmission of electric energy
cable designed for the purpose of transmission of signals
note: The term signal includes any type of signal, e.g. electric, optical, pneumatic etc.

definition: cable designed for use with baseband unmodulated signals
note: EXAMPLE...Audio,video and switching signals.

definition: cable designed for use with signals modulated on a radio-frequency carrier
conductor consisting of a single insulated conductive part
linear two-port device designed to introduce a desired delay in the transmission of a signal, without modifying the other characteristics of the signal
component built up of one or more two-terminal semiconductor devices and which can contain also resistors and/or capacitors
full-wave rectifier with four elements connected as a bridge circuit with dc voltage obtained from one pair of opposite junctions when alternating current is applied
two-terminal semiconductor device having an asymmetric voltage-current characteristic

two-terminal semiconductor device that operates in either an OFF (non-conducting) state or an ON (conducting) state, reaching the conducting state when a voltage of specified minimum value (breakover voltage) is applied across its terminals
semiconductor diode designed for rectification and including their associated mounting cooling attachments as integrated parts
diode used for the purpose of extracting or processing information contained in an electrical signal which varies with time and may be either analogue or digital

diode exhibiting a breakdown caused by the transition of electrons from the valence band to the conduction band due to tunnel action under the influence of a strong electric field in the reverse direction
diode that limit current to an essentially constant value over a specified voltage range
diode which develops a reference voltage of specified accuracy across its terminals, when biased to operate within a specified current range
diode which develops an essential constant voltage across its terminals throughout a specified current range

diode having closely controlled conductance, controlled storage charge and low leakage, as required for clippers, clamping circuits, bias regulators, and other logic circuits that require tight voltage-level tolerance
diode utilizing the steep slope of the blocking current-voltage characteristics of selenium plates for limiting transient overvoltages
note: Suppressors may be of two types: |Polarized: Selenium transient overvoltage suppressor having an asymmetrical current-voltage characteristic; |Non-polarized: Selenium transient overvoltage suppressor having a symmetrical current-voltage characteristic.

definition: diode that are designed that its capacitance can be varied by changing the voltage between its terminals
rectifier circuit capable of supplying a dc output voltage that is greater than (generally two or more times) the peak value of the ac input voltage

linear two-port device designed to transmit spectral components of signals according to a specified law, generally in order to pass the components in certain frequency bands and to attenuate those in other bands
filter that passes a specified range of frequencies and removes or attenuates the others
circuit in which all or some of the circuit elements are inseparably associated and electrically interconnected such that are considered to be indivisible for the purpose of construction and commerce
function designed to carry out operations on analogue signals

semiconductor device that accepts two analog inputs as non-inverting (+) input and inverting (-) input, then produces high or low voltage at a predefined level as output voltage depending on which input is higher
linear integrated circuit used for power line regulation
integrated circuit used in a power supply unit, of which voltage control element placed in series to the load
integrated circuit used in a power supply unit, of which voltage control element placed in parallel to the load

integrated circuit used in a power supply unit, which regulates the output voltage by an embedded switching circuit with its pulse width modulation (PWM) function or pulse frequency modulation(PFM) function
integrated circuit which monitors the circuit voltage level and sends a reset signal when it detects irregular voltage
function designed to carry out operations on digital signals
combinatorial/sequential interface function ICs
controller whose elements have been miniaturized into an integrated circuit
note: Microcontrollers are generally dedicated to a single application,mainly real time control.

definition: processor whose elements have been miniaturized into an integrated circuit
electronic component used to build reconfigurable digital circuits
note: NOTE 1...Unlike a logic gate, which has a fixed function, a programmable logic device has an undefined function at the time of manufacture. Before the programmable logic device can be used in a circuit it must be programmed, that is, reconfigured.| NOTE 2...A programmable logic device may also include registers.

definition: functional unit in which data (information) can be placed, in which they can be retained and from which they can be retrieved
memory that responds with all the data in a storage zone if a portion of that data matches the data used for addressing the memory
note: If a match could occur in more than one storage zone, then usually the data read out will be that contained in the storage zone having the lowest address value.

definition: charge-transfer device that stores charge in potential wells and transfers this charge almost completely as a packet by translating the position of the potential wells
note: This devices operate by changing the position of the same packet of charge.

definition: memory that permits access to any of its address locations in any desired sequence
memory in which the cells (elements) require the repetitive application of control signals in order to retain the data stored
note: A dynamic memory may use dynamic addressing and/or sensing circuits. This definition applies whether or not the control signals are generated inside or outside the memory.

definition: memory in which the data content is retained in the absence of control signals
note: A static memory may use dynamic addressing and/or sensing circuits.

definition: memory in which the content is intended to be read only and not to be altered during normal operation
one-dimensional arrangement of bistable circuits by means of which information may be accepted, stored, and retrieved
nonvolatile memory in which data can be erased generally in block or chip units and that can be written electrically
integrated circuit including basic logic circuits
function which involves analogue signals as well as digital signals
periodic/ DC function ICs
note: Periodic/ DC function indicates either periodic signals (signals f(x) of a real or complex variable with period T if f(x+T)= f(x) for every value of x) or DC signals (signals whose electric current flows in one direction only, as opposed to alternating current signal).

definition: two- or n-terminal device with the purpose to provide a defined inductance between any two terminals
remark: Many components used in electric or electronic circuits have a (mostly unwanted) inductance, e.g., the inductance of the connecting leads of a resistor. Such parasitic inductances are in most cases not the main purpose of the component and thus should not be taken as a rationale for classifying the component as an inductor.

definition: inductor designed so that its main properties cannot be varied
assembly of coils used to produce the magnetic fields for the deflection of one or more electron beams
an impedance transforming device for preventing energy within a given frequency band from taking an undesired path
set of series-connected turns of an electrical conductor
note: Coils may be used to introduce inductance into an electric circuit, to produce magnetic flux, or to react mechanically to a changing magnetic flux.

definition: device to adjust the variation of scanning speed during the trace interval to minimize geometric distortion
inductor through which antenna current flows
cylindrical coil used to produce a magnetic field to make a mechanical motion or force
inductor designed so that its main properties can be varied
source made in order to produce an optical radiation, usually visible
display made of material whose reflectance or transmittance changes when an electric field is applied
semiconductor device that emits or detects or that is responsive to coherent or non-coherent optical radiation or that utilises such radiation for its internal purposes
semiconductor optoelectronic device designed for the transfer of electric signals by utilizing radiant energy to provide coupling with electrical isolation between the input and the output
semiconductor optoelectronic device that directly converts electric energy into optical radiant energy
semiconductor diode, other than semiconductor lasers, capable of emitting visible radiation when excited by an electric current
semiconductor diode, other than semiconductor lasers, capable of emitting infrared radiation when excited by an electric current

semiconductor diode that emits coherent optical radiation through stimulated emission resulting from the recombination of free electrons and holes when excited by an electric current that exceeds the threshold current of the diode
semiconductor device that utilises the photoelectric effect for detection of optical radiation
photosensor operating with an optical radiation for which the wavelengths are longer than those for visible radiation
photosensor operating with an optical radiation for which the wavelengths are shorter than those for visible radiation
photosensor operating with any optical radiation capable of causing a visual sensation directly
unit that converts the incident optical (visible or invisible) image into the corresponding electrical image
active device producing a periodic quantity whose fundamental frequency is determined by the characteristics of the device
component that operates by the piezoelectric effect
note: The piezoelectric effect is a phenomenon in which electric polarization produces a mechanical deformation which depends on the electric field strength, and conversely a deformation produces an electric polarization.

definition: piezoelectric device that uses quartz crystal
resonator using quartz crystal and mounted in an enclosure
quartz crystal unit that operates in kHz frequency band
quartz crystal unit that operates in MHz frequency band
electrical device containing quartz crystal unit(s) and drive circuit(s) for frequency output
quartz crystal oscillator containing a crystal unit and a simple drive circuit
note: A simple packaged crystal oscillator has no means of temperature control or compensation.

definition: quartz crystal oscillator whose frequency deviation due to temperature is reduced by means of a implemented compensation system
quartz crystal oscillator, the frequency of which can be deviated or modulated according to a specified relation, by application of a control voltage
quartz crystal oscillator in which the temperature of the crystal unit is controlled with an oven, and the temperature of the crystal resonator is kept substantially constant
electrical filter in which one or more quartz crystal resonators are incorporated
device characterized essentially by its electric resistance
resistor with a designed inherent fixed resistance
note:
Since resistance is a function of temperature, it may still vary
with temperature.

definition: fixed resistor in which the voltage between the two terminals of the resistor is proportional to the current passing through it
fixed linear resistor constructed that each device contains only one fixed linear resistor
fixed resistor whose resistance tolerance is 5% or less
fixed resistor intended for applications over 10 W
fixed resistor intended for applications up to 10 W
fixed resistor intended for surface-mount applications
fixed resistor for use in thermostats
fixed resistor intended to fuse during the stated time at the specified overload current to protect the circuit
fixed linear resistor constructed that each device consists of, includes, or involves more than one fixed linear resistor
fixed resistor in which the voltage across the terminals is not proportional to the current passing through it
resistor constructed that the resistance value decreases when it is exposed to electromagnetic radiation
remark: The electromagnetic radiation is usually in the visible region of the spectrum.

definition: resistor in which the resistance value changes when the temperature is changed
resistor having a large non-linear negative temperature coefficient of resistance
resistor having a large non-linear positive temperature coefficient of resistance
resistor constructed that the resistance value has a very large variation depending on the applied voltage
resistor designed that its main property can be varied by mechanically changing the spatial relationship of its parts
variable resistor having one moving contact and one fixed terminal
note: EXAMPLE...rheostat

definition: resistor with one or more intermediate tappings allowing fractions of the voltage across the resistor to be obtained
potentiometer which can be mechanically adjusted but which is not intended for adjustment during normal operation
remark: A preset potentiometer is intended to provide a fixed resistance once the movable tapping has been set.

definition: potentiometer which can be mechanically adjusted by moving the adjuster along a linear path
potentiometer for which the mechanical adjustment is achieved by turning a shaft and whose position can be set with a high degree of precision
potentiometer for which the mechanical adjustment is achieved by turning a shaft and which is typically capable of dissipating powers of more than 10 W
potentiometer for which the mechanical adjustment is achieved by turning a shaft and which is typically able to dissipate powers up to 10 W
device which, when excited by a physical phenomenon, produces an electric signal characterizing the physical phenomenon
device for sensing, measuring and reporting the relative humidity in gas or matter
note: Humidity sensors are sensors operating on humidity, i.e. mass of water vapour divided by the volume of the gaseous mixture. Actual humidity by volume, divided by humidity by volume at saturation, at the same temperature.

definition: sensor operating on light; i.e. any optical radiation capable of causing a visual sensation directly
sensor operating on magnetic fields
sensor operating on radiation released during reactions taking place in atomic nuclei
sensor operating on pressure; i.e. force divided by area
sensor operating on proximity; i.e. nearness in space
sensor operating on temperature; i.e.intensity of heat
converter that transforms, by electromagnetic induction, a system of alternating voltage and current into another system of voltage and current at the same frequency
note: In normal conditions of use, the secondary voltage and current are proportional to the primary voltage and current.

definition: transformer designed for transmitting electrical power
note: In most cases power transformers are designed for one or more well defined frequencies, such as 50 Hz or 60 Hz.

definition: power transformer designed that its main properties cannot be varied
power transformer designed that its main properties can be varied
note: The main properties may be varied by modifying the spatial relationship of its parts.

definition: transformer designed to convert a system of alternating voltage and current representing information into another voltage and current preserving the information content represented by the original system of voltages and currents
note: In most cases signal transformers are designed to cope with broader frequency ranges than, e.g., power transformers, such as 20 Hz to 20 kHz or 20 Hz to 11 MHz.

definition: signal transformer designed that its main properties cannot be varied
signal transformer designed that its main properties can be varied
note: The main properties may be varied by modifying the spatial relationship of its parts.

definition: semiconductor device capable of providing power amplification and having three or more terminals
transistor having at least two junctions and whose functioning depends on both minority carriers and majority carriers (of opposite electric charge)
bipolar transistor, designed for power-signal applications, having a thermal resistance between junction and mounting base of 15 K/W or less
bipolar power transistor designed for use with baseband unmodulated signals
note: EXAMPLE...Audio, video and switching signals

definition: bipolar power transistor designed for use with signals modulated on a radio-frequency carrier
bipolar transistor, designed for small-signal applications, having a thermal resistance between junction and mounting base of more than 15 K/W
bipolar small signal transistor designed for use with baseband unmodulated signals
note: EXAMPLE...Audio,video and switching signals.

definition: bipolar small signal transistor designed for use with signals modulated on a radio-frequency carrier
transistor in which the current flowing through a conductor channel is controlled by an electric field arising from a voltage applied between the gate and source terminals
field-effect transistor designed for power-signal applications, having a thermal resistance between junction and mounting base of 15 K/W or less
field-effect power transistor designed for use with baseband unmodulated signals
note: EXAMPLE...Audio,video and switching signals.

definition: field-effect transistor designed for use with signals modulated on a radio-frequency carrier
field-effect transistor designed for small-signal applications, having a thermal resistance between junction and mounting base of more than 15 K/W

transistor having a conduction channel and a PN junction, of which the currents flowing through the channel and the junction are controlled by an electric field resulting from a voltage applied between the gate and emitter terminals
bistable semiconductor device comprising three or more junctions which can be switched from the off-state to the on-state or vice versa
two-terminal trigger device having substantially the same behaviour in the first and third quadrants of the principal characteristic
three-terminal trigger device having a different behaviour in the first and third quadrants of the principal characteristic
fast turn-off thyristor
thyristor which can be switched from the on-state to the off-state and vice versa by applying control signals of appropriate polarity to the gate terminal
unidirectional thyristor that switch for positive anode voltages and exhibits a reverse blocking state for negative anode voltages
bidirectional triode thyristor
note: Triacs are bi-directional triode thyristors: three-terminal trigger-devices having substantially the same switching behaviour in the first and third quadrants of behaviour in the first and third quadrants of the principal characteristic.

definition: electronic device in which conduction takes place by electrons or ions between electrodes through a vacuum or gaseous medium within a gas tight envelope, but excluding devices used only for lighting
signal-to-image converter tube in which a well defined and controllable beam of electrons is produced and directed on to a surface to give a visible or otherwise detectable display or effect
display tube which can produce images in colour by varying the relative intensity of excitation of three screen phosphors of different chromaticity
display tube which can produce images of only one chromaticity,usually determined by a screen phosphor with a single pair of colour coordinates
electronic tube in which the electrical characteristics are substantially established by the ionisation of deliberately introduced gas or vapour
tube the functioning of which is determined by the photoelectric effect
electronic tube whose mode of operation is based on the control of space-charge-limited current by electrode voltages
vacuum tube the functioning of which is based on the conversion of energy by interaction of electromagnetic fields with space-charge waves of an electron beam

packaged unit capable of producing only the first portion of the functions of a receiver and delivering either radio-frequency, intermediate-frequency, or demodulated information to some other equipment

component for handling radio waves with a wavelength sufficiently low to allow use of technologies such as waveguides, cavities, or planar transmission lines; the corresponding frequencies are higher than about 1 GHz
note: The term microwave generally refers to the frequency range where circuits and device interconnects are described as distributed elements instead of lumped elements.

definition: piece of insulating material with fixed metal traces to connect electronic components
note: Printed circuit boards are typically subdivided according to:, - their structure (e.g. single- and double-sided, multilayers), - the nature of the base material (e.g. rigid, flexible).

definition: branch of optics that deals with the transmission of light through transparent fibers
link that consists of a end-to-end optical circuit
note: EXAMPLE A cable of optical fiber, possibly concatenated into a dark fiber link, is the simplest form of an optical link.

definition: mechanical or optical device that provides a demountable connection between two fibers or a fiber and a source or detector
optical element to switch optical signals without converting into electric signals
fibre optic branch
optical device that combines or splits power from optical fibers
passive device for reducing the amplitude of a signal without appreciably distorting the waveform
opto-electric transducer used to convert optical power to electrical current
optical component which allows the transmission of light in only one direction
data communication network built with optical fiber technology
light sources for use in fibre optic systems
fibre optic device which is used to modulate a beam of light
device functioning as an electrical-to-optical converter including a source/ detector and signal processing electronics
physical structure that guides electromagnetic waves in the optical spectrum
A set of fibre optic cables for which each cable can be described by the same set of data element types
optical fiber instrument used for wavelength selection, which can select desired wavelengths to pass and reject the others
lenses for use with fibre optic systems
device with two or more electrodes between which an electric discharge is initiated under specified conditions
spark gap where the electrodes are separated by air
spark gap where the electrodes are separated by a gas other than air
A set of resonators of which each can be described with the same group of data element types.
resonator that uses surface acoustic wave (SAW) characteristics for having resonance
SAW resonator that has one input port and one output port
SAW resonator that has two input ports and two output ports
note: A two port SAW has in general a wider range of resonance than a one port SAW.

definition: display device in which the electrical drive excites an electrical discharge in the gas within the device
component utilizing electromagnetic energy of which the operation involves mechanical motion
component which terminates one or more conductors for the purpose of providing connection and disconnection to a suitable mating component
connector with circular shell or housing
connector with contacts in a rectangular or near-rectangular array
connector intended for use with printed-circuit boards
connector intended for use at radio frequencies
A set of connectors intended for use with IC cards for which each connector is described by the same set of data element types
connector consisting of one or more connector modules each providing a set of links
electrical connectors consisting of plugs (male-ended) and jacks (female-ended)
electrical connectors consisting of plugs and jacks of cylindical shape
plugs of cylindical shape
A set of concentric jacks for which each jack is described by the same set of data element types
arrangement of cylindrical jacks
connectors using conductive pins (round or rectangular) for making connections
plug using conductive pins (round or rectangular) for making connections
receptacle for pin plugs
arrangement of pin jacks
receptacle for pin plugs designed to prevent the radiation of electromagnetic interference to and from the internal conductor(s)

preferred_name: plug and jack for dc power
definition: plug and jack for use in dc power applications
plug for use in dc power applications
jack for use in dc power applications
plug for use in dc power applications in automobiles
the fitting together of manufactured parts into a plug or an arragement of plugs
the fitting together of manufactured parts into a jack or an arragement of jacks
female receptacle (typically socket contacts) of a plug and socket connector
holder for mounting or connecting to vacuum tubes
holder for mounting or connecting to transistors
holder for mounting or connecting to integrated circuits
holder for mounting or connecting to PC boards
device that allows electrically operated equipment to be connected to the primary power supply
device that allows to connect information transmitting links
holder for mounting or connecting to quartz crystals
holder for mounting or connecting equipment intended for use in lighting applications such as lamps or lightbulbs
socket or holder for mounting and connecting to fuses
socket for connection to antenna feeders
conductive part of a device, electric circuit or electric network, provided for connecting that device, electric circuit or electric network to one or more external conductors
terminals which typically only have the facility for connecting one, two or three conductors
arrangement of multiple terminals
insulated board equipped with multiple terminals, which enables the interconnection of wires or cables
note: Such terminals may be, for instance, in the form of binding posts or lugs.

definition: rod serving as an terminal, such as a copper-clad steel rod, solid copper rod, or galvanized steel rod
note: EXAMPLE Earthing rod, air-terminal rod.

definition: device that, by the fusing of one or more of their specially-designed and proportioned components, opens the circuit in which they is inserted by breaking the current when this exceeds a given value for a sufficient time
note: The fuse comprises all the parts that form the complete device.

definition: electrical safety device that interrupts electric current when exposed to excess current
electrical safety device that interrupts electric current when heated to a specific temperature
transducer by which acoustic waves are obtained from electric oscillation waves and designed to radiate acoustic power into the surrounding medium
loudspeaker that operates by the motion of a conductor or a coil, carrying a varying current, in a steady magnetic field
loudspeaker that operates by the motion of a magnet attached to a diaphragm and activated by a current through a fixed coil
loudspeaker that operates by the interaction between an ionic plasma and the surrounding air
loudspeaker that operates by the motion of a magnet, connected to the membrane, and actuated by a variable magnetic field
loudspeaker that operates by controlled variations of an air stream
loudspeaker that operates by the piezoelectric properties of the material
loudspeaker that operates by electrostatic forces
loudspeaker that operates by the magnetostrictive properties of a material
electroacoustical transducer by which electric signals are obtained from acoustical oscillations
electric) machine which converts electric energy into mechanical energy
electric motor that has been split and unrolled into two flat sheets, that the motion between rotor and stator is linear rather than rotary
linear motor designed essentially for application with an alternating current or voltage
linear motor designed essentially for application with a dc current or dc voltage
linear motor, the rotor of which rotates in angular increments when the stator windings are energised in a programmed manner
linear motor which can be operated by either direct current or single phase alternating current of normal supply frequencies
motor characterized by turning about an axis
rotational motor designed essentially for application with an alternating current or voltage
rotational motor designed essentially for application with a dc current or voltage
rotational motor the rotor of which rotates in angular increments when the stator windings are energised in a programmed manner
rotational motor which can be operated by either direct current or single phase alternating current of normal supply frequencies

electric) device designed to close or open one or more electric circuits by means of separate contacts, after the appearance of certain conditions in the electric input circuits controlling the device
device designed to close and open one or more electric circuits by means of separable contacts, under control of an external quantity
switch that has contacts mounted on ferromagnetic reeds (thin bars) sealed in a glass tube, designed for activation by an external magnetic field
switch actuated by an external mechanical force

switch whose function is controlled by variations of temperature and whose contacts make or break automatically a load circuit when the temperature of the ambient space in which their sensing element is placed or the temperature of the surface on which they are fixed reaches a predetermined value
component which may be used to assemble complete connectors, which is used as accessories to a connector or which is needed in the assembly or disassembly of connectors
conducting part within a connector which mate individually to form the electrical connection
component which may be used with connectors to facilitate their use in a wide range of applications
note: Accessories perform no electrical function and include such items as caps,covers,gaskets washers and boots.

definition: tool which is needed for the assembly, disassembly, installation or repair of connectors
note: They include such items as spanners, keys, jigs and gauges.

definition: external housing of connectors which normally provides the mechanical mating
parts formed from insulating material providing the mechanical support for the connector contacts within the connector shell
product consisting entirely of the same magnetic material except for possible coating or finishing of the surface
magnetic part having a high coercivity
magnetic part having a low coercivity
product intended for further physical processing including chemical and nuclear processing during which their geometry or composition will change
material related to containing, proceeding, arising from, actuated by, or carrying sound
material exhibiting ferromagnetism
material exhibiting ferromagnetism having a high coercivity
material exhibiting ferromagnetism having a low coercivity
material which are transparent to light or to infra- red, ultra violet or X-ray radiation, such as glass and certain single crystals, polycrystalline
material that can be used to convert thermal energy into electric energy or provides refrigeration directly from electric energy
copper-clad laminate for printed circuits
substances which permit very less current flow through them
autonomous and stand-alone abstraction of an object of the parts/component library application domain
note: A feature might be represented as a specific subclass of other classes.

definition: value for physical quantities that may be expressed in a Cartesian form with a real and an imaginary part or in a polar form with a magnitude and an argument (phase angle)
ratio of voltage to current expressed as a complex number value
collection of properties representing the admittance of an electric component as a complex quantity of which each set of values can be described by the same set of data element types
note: ADMITTANCE is the ratio of current to voltage expressed as a complex number value

definition: value for physical quantities that may be expressed as the nominal value of the quantity together with deviations from that nominal value as percentages or absolute values
capacitance of an electric component together with associated tolerances
resistance of an electric component together with associated tolerances
physical, chemical or biological conditions, external to a product, to which it is subjected at a certain time
note: NOTE 1  Environmental conditions are generally composed of environmental conditions appearing in nature and environmental conditions generated by the product itself or by external sources ||, NOTE 2 The characteristics specified allow judgment about |, - possibly required additional shelters for objects in order to operate in this environment, or |, -  to take appropriate other measures to ensure the suitability of the object for operating in this environment

definition: climatic conditions related to the the degree of ambient heat
degree of wetness of the atmosphere
pressure exerted by the atmosphere as a consequence of gravitational attraction
constituents of atmosphere
place where the ambient conditions apply
conditions that apply to rain, snow, hail, or volcanic ashes
phenomena related to the emission of radiant energy
movement of surrounding air or water including movement of the object in relation to the surrounding medium
thunderstorm related conditions
ambient contamination produced by nature or by industrial labour
chemical erosion by motionless or moving agents
ambient conditions caused by flora or fauna
ambient conditions established through the presence of substances in gaseous, liquid or solid form that may react with the component in question
ambient conditions established through moving particles
ambient conditions related to the existence of explosive gazes and vapours
ambient conditions related to the existence of flammable particles

preferred_name: water from other sources than rain
definition: water related conditions except precipation
remark: Include splashing, spraying water, water jets, waves, immersion, or submersion

definition: conditions related to the conversion of the surrounding air from gaseous state into liquid state
periodical or non-periodical movements or oscillations of the ambience excluding seismic activities
periodical or non-periodical movements or oscillations of the ambience caused by seismic activities
ambient conditions caused by shock or stroke
ambient conditions related to the existence of unwanted electric or magnetic fields
ambient conditons related to undesired acoustic sounds
characteristics related to the presence of water within the ambience of the given location
characteristics related to the suitability of the soil for construction work

preferred_name: potable and other types of water
definition: characteristics to specify the suitability of water for some purpose
information set specifying alternative identification numbers for an entity
note: The listed identification numbers may belong to one or several domains or sub-domains.

definition: solid three-dimensional geometric shape, which can be easily generated using parameters without applying special features, such as extruding or revolving
note: Constructive solid geometry primitives can be used as an operands of a Boolean set operation to generate more a complex shape as a result of the operation.

definition: solid rectangular parallelepiped, specified by a location and a placement coordinate system
note: NOTE 1  The block is specified by the positive lengths x, y, and z along the axes of the placement coordinate system, and has one vertex at the origin of the placement coordinate system.||NOTE 2  The property axis2_placement3d specifies the local coordinate system of the block whereas the property location specifies the position of the origin of the local coordinate system within the geometric context.

definition: solid pyramid with a rectangular base whose apex is directly above the centre point of the base
note: The location and orientation of the pyramid defines a placement coordinate system for the pyramid. The pyramid has one corner of its base at its location and the edges of the base are aligned with the first two placement axes in the positive sense.||NOTE 2  The property axis2_placement3d specifies the local coordinate system of the rectangular pyramid whereas the property location specifies the position of the origin of the local coordinate system within the geometric context.

definition: solid with two triangular/ trapezoidal faces created by intersecting a block with a plane perpendicular to one of its faces
note: The trapezoidal faces may be degenerated to triangular ones.||NOTE 2  The property axis2_placement3d specifies the local coordinate system of the right angular wedge whereas the property location specifies the position of the origin of the local coordinate system within the geometric context.

definition: solid cylinder of finite height defined by an axis point at the centre of one planar circular face, an axis, a height, and the radius of the circular faces
note: NOTE 1  The location of the cylinder is a point on the axis of the cylinder and at the centre of one of the planar circular faces.||NOTE 2  The axis of the cylinder has the direction of the central axis of symmetry of the cylinder.||NOTE 3  The property axis1_placement3d specifies the local coordinate system of the right circular cylinder whereas the property location specifies the position of the origin of the local coordinate system within the geometric context.

definition: solid in the form of a cone that may be truncated and defined by an axis, a point on the axis, the semi-angle of the cone, and a distance giving the location in the negative direction along the axis from the point to the base of the cone
note: NOTE 1  A radius is given, which, if nonzero, gives the size and location of a truncated face of the cone.||NOTE 2  The location of the cone is a point on the axis of the cone and on one of the planar circular faces, or, if radius is zero, at the apex.||NOTE 3  The property axis1_placement3d specifies the local coordinate system of the right circular cone whereas the property location specifies the position of the origin of the local coordinate system within the geometric context.

definition: solid with a spherical surface defined by a centre and a radius
note: NOTE 1  The property sphere center specifies the position of the sphere within the geometric context.

definition: solid defined by sweeping the area of a circle (the generatrix) along a larger circle (the directrix)
note: NOTE 1  The major radius shall be greater than the minor radius.||NOTE 2  The location of the central point on the axis and the direction of the axis define the centre and plane of the directrix.||NOTE 3  The property axis1_placement3d specifies the local coordinate system of the torus whereas the property location specifies the position of the origin of the local coordinate system within the geometric context.

definition: point defined by its coordinates in a rectangular Cartesian coordinate system
note: For the purpose of defining solid geometry three-dimensional points are used.

definition: information useable to unambigiously contact an individual or an organization using phone or mail or other means to communicate
generic concept for the use of an internationally registered identification system for objects
generic concept for the use of a nationally registered identification system for objects
generic concept for the use of a name-based organization-bound identification system for objects
geographical location where individual people and organizations are residing according to an address system
note: The physical address provides a means to lookup, visit or pickup items at a geographical location belonging to people and organizations.

definition: designation of a location in an electronic network
note: The electronic address provides a means to connect to people, organizations and systems via electronic communication media.

definition: designation of a delivery point according to a postal service
note: The postal address provides a means to send physical items to people and organizations by means of postal services.

definition: information set for the identification of an individual human being
concept for the identification of a specific object formed with respect to the system of which the object is a constituent, based on one or more aspects of that system
remark: An object may have more than one object reference designation in accordance with the used aspects

definition: concept for the identification of a terminal with respect to the object to which it belongs, related to one defined aspect
remark: A terminal may have more than one terminal reference designation in accordance with the used aspects

definition: concept for the association of an object to a class within a documented classification system
remark: An object may be multiply classified if different classification systems are applied

definition: concept for the identification of a signal connection chain within a given domain conveying information from one object to another
note: Messages (units of signals) may be sent in a communication network in the form of telegrams. Such messages may represent one or several signals.

definition: concept for the identification of a section of a group of coherent signal connections belonging to the same signal on the way from its source to its destination
concept for the declaration of materials in products
note: a material declaration may occur multiple times due to the number of materials to be declared

definition: statement on one or more products providing detailed materials content information or indicating compliance with related requiremens
material declaration information set compliant with IEC 62474:2012 for products of companies operating in and supplying the electrotechnical industry
information about the material composition of products used in electrotechnical industry for compliance and environmentally conscious design requirements
group of one or more classes with properties capable of specifying facts on material declaration
any goods or service
note: This general definition of product is in the context of this International Standard limited to any product of the product category “hardware” according to ISO 9000:2005 No. 3.4.2 of and for the electrotechnical and electronic industry (E&E).

definition: sub-unit of a product or another (product) part
sequence of characters unambigiously identifying a product, being administered by a known authority
mass expressed as the nominal value of the quantity together with deviations from that nominal value

mass of a constituent as a percent (%) of the total mass of all constituents as specified in the declaration requirements expressed as the nominal value of the quantity together with deviations from that nominal value
defined classification of materials that are established in referenced IEC 62474:2012 database for purposes of inventorying aspects of a product, such that no two classes contain the same materials
substance or mixture within a product or product part
one or more substances, where in the case of multiple substances they share at least one chemical sub-structure, or chemical or physical property under a generic name

a chemical element and its compounds in the natural state or obtained by any manufacturing process, including any additive necessary to preserve its stability and any impurity deriving from the process used, but excluding any solvent which may be separated without affecting the stability of the substance or changing its composition
source_document: Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling (GHS):2003, 1.2

definition: collection of exemption statements applicable to a product being declared
statement indicating the state of being free or freed from a charge or obligation to which others are subject
note: EXAMPLE Exemptions to RoHS are granted to narrowly-defined applications for which the elimination of the prohibited substance is technically or scientifically impracticable or when the only available substitution produces more negative than positive benefits to the environment, health, or consumer safety.

definition: information specifying supporting information added to the declaration
distinctive designation identifying a relevant item, being administered by a known authority

preferred_name: declaration of waste of electrical and electronic equipment
definition: information set related to the directive on waste of electrical and electronic equipment of the European Union
note: NOTE: Declaration according to the EU directive 2012/19/EU, WEEE.

preferred_name: declaration of restriction of the use of certain hazardous substances
definition: information set related to the directive on the restriction of the use of certain hazardous substances in electrical and electronic equipment of the European Union
note: NOTE: Declaration according to the EU directive 2002/95/EC, RoHS.

preferred_name: declaration of registration, evaluation, authorisation and restriction of chemicals
definition: information set related to registration, evaluation, authorisation and restriction of chemicals of the European Union
note: NOTE: Declaration according to REACH.

definition: concept describing the situation when a (serial) product, process or service is no longer in the focus of marketing, no longer available in stock or is no longer manufactured
note: a phase-out process is planned and announced in time to allow customers to prepare for the succeeding product or product version or other modifications

definition: collection of basic concepts describing airborne sound radiated by a well-defined noise source, for example a machine under test, under specified operating and mounting conditions
note: emission values may be incorporated in a product label and/or in a product specification. In countries or regions noise emission data may be requested by law, e.g. Noise Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC, Outdoor Noise Directive 2000/14/EC, Machinery Directive 98/37/EC

definition: physical, chemical or biological conditions, external to a product, to which it may be subjected during transportation, including precautionary measures
note:
NOTE 1 Environmental conditions are generally composed of environmental conditions appearing in nature and environmental conditions generated by the product itself or by external sources

NOTE 2 The characteristics specified allow judgment about
- possibly required additional shelters for objects in order to be transported in this environment, or
- to take appropriate other measures to ensure the suitability of the object for transportation

definition: physical, chemical or biological conditions, external to a product, to which it may be subjected during storage, including precautionary measures
note:
NOTE 1 Environmental conditions are generally composed of environmental conditions appearing in nature and environmental conditions generated by the product itself or by external sources

NOTE 2 The characteristics specified allow judgment about
- possibly required additional shelters for objects in order to be stored in this environment, or
- to take appropriate other measures to ensure the suitability of the object for storage

definition: elements for the collection and analysis of numerical data in large quantities for measuring, controlling and communicating uncertainty, especially for the purpose of inferring proportions in a whole from those in a representative sample
collection of characteristics applied in probability distributions
conditions under which a given object shall be physically installed for its operation according to the manufacturer's specification
preparation of an object or commodity for its proper storage and transportation
remark: packing can entail activities such as blocking, bracing, cushioning, marking, sealing, strapping, weather proofing, or wrapping.

definition: set of describing properties of the object
concept of referencing to specification documents used in the design, manufacturing, testing, quality, safety etc. of an object
difference between input power and output power of a device
note: If the output power and/or input power is electric, active power is meant.
remark:
1 This collection provides a overview of power losses of a component during all operational stages defined in IEC 62744, independent whether the power consumption is caused by unintended losses or whether it is intended, e.g. for radiation or heating purposes.

2  The properties can be applied also for calculation of the energy efficiency of a component being defined by: ratio or other quantitative relationship between an output of performance, service, goods or energy, and an input of energy 
[SOURCE: ISO 50001:2011, 3.8]

3 IEC 61439-1 provides guidance to the calculation of power losses in closed assemblies, e.g. cubicles, when having the losses of the assembled components.

definition: systematic examination of the extent to which a product, process or service fulfils specified requirements
individual or organization responsible for registering an object of interest, e.g. a product
body that performs conformity assessment services
note: An accreditation body is not a conformity assessment body.

definition: collection of properties providing information about the attestation of a conformity assessment that fulfilment of specified requirements has been demonstrated
note: The resulting statement conveys the assurance that the specified requirements have been fulfilled. Such an assurance does not, of itself, afford contractual or other legal guarantees.

definition: concept providing information about the validity associated with items
collection of properties providing information about the effectivity associated to items with respect to defined points in time.
remark:
Example: period of time for which something, e.g. a certificate, is current, that lasts from the date on which it becomes effective (“effective date”), resulting from a decision of the body responsible for it, until it is withdrawn or replaced
ISO/IEC Guide 2:2004, 9.3, mod

definition: collection of properties providing information about the effectivity associated to items with respect to time periods
collection of properties providing information about the effectivity associated to items with respect to geografical regions.
note: regions are identified by the 2-letter country code in accordance with ISO 3166-1

definition: collection of properties providing information about the effectivity associated to items with respect to organizations
collection of properties providing information about domains other than geografical regions and organizations where the item becomes valid
collection of properties providing information about date and time of the issued document

concept describing the promises about a product made by, either the manufacturer or its representant, including the applying conditions for product faults or defects, which is a part of the sales contract
note:
1 implied legal warrenty is imposed by local or regional law in a sale.
2 contractual warrenty applies independently of the implied legal warrenty.
3 consumer or industrial goods are considered to be of the non-wearable type. Both good types may contain wearable goods with their own periods of warranty.
remark: manufacturer commits himself for a defined period of time to correct defects, or exchange defective products, irrespective of whether the defect has been existent or not at the time of delivery of the object of purchase.

definition: concept describing the ability of an item to perform as and when required
note:
Note 1 Dependability includes availability, reliability, recoverability, maintainability, and maintenance support performance, and, in some cases, other characteristics such as durability, safety and security. 
Note 2 Dependability is used as a collective term for the time-related quality characteristics of an item.

definition: generic concept of describing the ability of an item to perform as required, under given conditions of use and maintenance, until the end of useful life
note: 1 Besides generic information, item clases may have item-specific durability related information elements

definition: concept describing generic after-sales related performance characteristics of the supplier influencing the usability of the item
note: Note 1 For more time-related quality characteristics of an item see ADA137., Note 2 For warrenty issues see ADA112

definition: concept of measures for describing the reliability of an item
ability to perform as required, without failure, for a given time interval, under given conditions
note:
Note 1 The time interval duration can be expressed in units appropriate to the item concerned, e.g. calendar time, operating cycles, distance run, etc., and the units should always be clearly stated. 

Note 2 Given conditions include aspects that affect reliability, such as: mode of operation, stress levels, environmental conditions, and maintenance.

Note 3 Reliability can be quantified using measures defined in Section IEV 192-05, Reliability related concepts: measures.

definition: ability to be in a state to perform as required
note:
Note 1 Availability depends upon the combined characteristics of the reliability (IEV 192-01-24), recoverability (IEV 192-01-25), and maintainability (IEV192-01-27) of the item, and the maintenance support performance (IEV192-01-29). 

Note 2 Availability may be quantified using measures defined in Section IEV 192-08, Availability related measures.

definition: ability to recover from a failure, without corrective maintenance
note:
Note 1 The ability to recover may or may not require external actions. For recovery where no external actions are required, see self-recoverability (IEV 192-01-26). 

Note 2 Recoverability may be quantified using measures such as the probability of recovery within a specified time interval.

definition: ability to be retained in, or restored to a state to perform as required, under given conditions of use and maintenance
note:
Note 1 Given conditions would include aspects that affect maintainability, such as: location for maintenance, accessibility, maintenance procedures and maintenance resources.

Note 2 Maintainability may be quantified using measures defined in Section IEV 192-07, Maintainability and maintenance support: measures.

definition: effectiveness of an organization in respect of maintenance support
note: Note 1 Maintenance support performance may be quantified using measures defined in Section IEV 192-07, Maintainability and maintenance support: measures.

preferred_name: measures for maintainability and maintenance support related concepts
definition: concept of measures for describing the maintainability and maintenance support of an item
concept of measures for describing the availability of an item
generic concept of forecasting preventive maintenance needs of an item in service by mathematical or statistical methods, based on condition monitoring about physical state or operational parameter
note:
1  May be conducted automatically during operation or at planned time intervals

2 Result may lead to an unscheduled maintenance, whereas a scheduled maintenance is carried in accordance with a s specified time schedule

definition: autonomous and stand-alone abstraction of an object of the parts/component library application domain concerning shape, configuration, or arrangement
bare semiconductor die or wafer, with or without connection structures, or minimally-packaged dies or wafers
unpackaged discrete semiconductor or integrated circuit in the form of singulated die or sawn or unsawn wafers with pads on one surface suitable for interconnection to the substrate or package
unpackaged die or wafer that has had added bumps for interconnection and/or mechanical attachment
note: Typically these can be die that have had solder or other metallic bumps added to the metallised pads on the die (also known as flip-chip).

definition: unpackaged die that has had added lead frames or similar terminations connected to the pads on the die to allow interconnection and/or mechanical attachment
die or wafer that has had some exterior packaging medium and interconnection structure added for protection and ease of handling
bare die devices having connections on both surfaces
geometric description of the physical shape of a packaged component including its dimensions
package style in which the body is spherical or almost spherical
leads extending outwards from the package along one of its axes in opposite directions away from the centre of the package
bead package outline style with axial wire leads
bead package outline style with straight axial wire leads
lead which emerges from the face of a package which is on or nearest to its mounting plane
bead package outline style with bottom wire leads
bead package outline style with straight bottom wire leads
bead package outline style with formed bottom wire leads
package style in which the body has a rectangular in shape and has very short connections or connections on the surface
lead that is disposed around all four sides of a rectangular package

quad chip-carrier package outline style with standard J-bend leads
quad non-bumped chip-carrier package outline style with standard J-bend leads
A set of quad leadless chip-carrier package outline styles of which each outline style can be described with the same group of data element types
quad leadless chip-carrier package outline style with standard leads
quad leadless chip-carrier non-cavity package outline style with standard leads
quad leadless chip-carrier cavity package outline style with standard leads
package style in which the body is cylindrical with the length no smaller than the diameter
lead that extends outwards from the package along one of its axes in opposite directions away from the centre of the package
cylindrical package outline style with axial wire leads
cylindrical package outline style with straight axial wire leads
plain cylindrical package outline style with straight axial wire leads
top-hat cylindrical package outline style with straight axial wire leads
lead which emerges from the face of a package which is on or nearest to its mounting plane
cylindrical package outline style with bottom wire leads
cylindrical package outline style with bottom wire leads on a circle
A set of cylindrical package outline styles with straight in-line bottom wire leads of which each outline style can be described with the same group of data element types
cylindrical package outline style with formed in-line bottom wire leads
cylindrical package outline style with bottom wire leads on a square grid
cylindrical package outline style with offset bottom wire leads
connection formed on or around the ends of a package
cylindrical package outline style with wrap-around end connections
cylindrical package outline style with wrap-around end connections in standard form
lead exrending outwards from a package in a direction perpendicular to one of the principal axes of the package
cylindrical package outline style with radial solder-lug leads
A set of cylindrical package outline styles with straight radial solder-lug leads of which each outline style can be described with the same group of data element types
cylindrical package outline style with radial wire leads
cylindrical package outline style with straight radial wire leads
connection which emerges from the face of a package which is opposite to the face seated on the mounting plane
cylindrical package outline style with upper solder-lug connections
cylindrical package outline style with upper solder-lug connections in standard form
cylindrical clamp-mount package outline style with upper solder-lug connections in standard form
cylindrical stud-mount package outline style with upper solder-lug connections in standard form
cylindrical package outline style with upper screw connections
cylindrical package outline style with upper screw connections in standard form
cylindrical clamp-mount package outline style with upper screw connections in standard form
cylindrical stud-mount package outline style with upper screw connections in standard form
package style in which the body is cylindrical with the length smaller than the diameter
lead extending outwards from the package along one of its axes in opposite directions away from the centre of the package
disc package outline style with axial wire leads
disc package outline style with straight axial wire leads
lead which emerges from the face of a package which is on or nearest to its mounting plane
disc package outline style with bottom wire leads
disc package outline style with straight bottom wire leads
disc package outline style with formed bottom wire leads
package style which is intended for mounting by flanges which form part of the package
lead which emerges from the face of a package which is on or nearest to its mounting plane
flange-mount package outline style with bottom pins
flange-mount package outline style with offset bottom pins
flange-mount package outline style with bottom pins on a circle
lead which is disposed in pairs in two parallel rows
A set of flange-mount package outline styles with a double row of pins of which each outline style can be described with the same group of data element types
flange-mount package outline style with a double row of round pins
lead which are disposed in a single row along one side of a package
flange-mount package outline style with a single row of through-hole leads
flange-mount package outline style with a single row of straight, flat, through-hole leads
flange-mount package outline style with a single row of straight, V-section, through-hole leads
lead which is disposed in two parallel rows with each lead in a row opposite the space between two leads in the other row
flange-mount package outline style with through-hole leads in a zig-zag pattern
flange-mount package outline style with straight, flat, through-hole leads in a zig-zag pattern
flange-mount package outline style with straight, V-section, through-hole leads in a zig-zag pattern
package style which is intended for mounting and connection by leads extending away from the body in a horizontal plane
lead which is disposed in a two parallel rows
dual flat-pack package outline style with flat leads
dual flat-pack package outline style with straight, flat leads
lead that is disposed around all four sides of a rectangular package
A set of quad flat-pack package outline styles with gull-wing leads of which each outline style can be described with the same group of data element types
A set of quad flat-pack package outline styles with gull-wing leads in standard form of which each outline style can be described with the same group of data element types
quad flat-pack non-bumped package outline style with gull-wing leads in standard form
package style in which the body has a rectangular shape with leads disposed in regular patterns over one face
lead which emerges from the face of a package which is on or nearest to its mounting plane
A set of grid-array package outline styles with bottom butt leads of which each outline style can be described with the same group of data element types
grid-array package outline style with bottom solder balls
rigid pin which is disposed over the face of a package and perpendicular to it
pin-grid-array package outline style with pin leads
pin-grid-array package outline style with round pins
pin-grid-array cavity-up package outline style with round pins
pin-grid-array cavity-down package outline style with round pins

lead which is disposed in a single row along one side of a package
A set of triple-in-line package outline styles with through-hole leads of which each outline style can be described with the same group of data element types

lead which is disposed in two parallel rows with each lead in a row opposite the space between two leads in the other row
zigzag-in-line package outline style with through-hole leads
zigzag-in-line package outline style with through-hole leads in standard form

leads extending outwards from the package along one of its axes in opposite directions away from the centre of the package
long-form package outline style with axial wire leads
A set of long-form package outline styles with straight axial wire leads of which each outline style can be described with the same group of data element types
package style in which the package is mounted and fastened by a post or stud
connection which emerges from the face of a package which is opposite to the face seated on the mounting plane

post-mount package outline style with one upper solder-lug connection
